figures.caption: FIG. 2. Brillouin zones for the (i) 0-flux (ε = 1), (ii) π-flux (ε = 2), and (iii) π/2-flux (ε = 4) states. Here, ε denotes the enlargement factor of the parton Brillouin zone: the parton primitive cell is spanned by e1, εe2, and εe3, where e1,2,3 are the FCC lattice vectors defined in Eq. (2).

TABLE VI. The 12 U(1) spin singlet states at the NN bond level (non-chiral/chiral), classified by the U(1) fluxes on the diamond plaquettes ϕ , triangle plaquettes ϕ / , and hexagon plaquettes ϕ . a (0, 0, 0): This Ansatz is labelled as [0, 0, 0] in Ref. [41], and referred to as the uniform state in Ref. [40]. b (π, 0, π): This Ansatz is labelled as [0, 0, π] in Ref. [41], and as (π, π) in Table I of Ref. [40]. c (0, π/2, 0): This Ansatz is referred to as the monopole flux state in Ref. [40]. In Ref. [41], this state is labelled as [π 2 , π 2 , 0] and is referred to as the uniform flux state. d (π, π/2, π): This Ansatz is labelled as (π/2, π) in Table I of Ref. [40]. e (π, π/2, 0): This Ansatz is referred to as the staggered flux state and labelled [π 2 ,−π 2 , 0] in Ref. [41].

FIG. 6. One-dimensional cuts of the equal-time spin structure factors for the eight unprojected U(1) chiral spin-liquid Ansätze, taken along the two principal directions through the pinch point and along a representative high-symmetry path. The data are normalized such that ∑ q S(q) = 1.

FIG. 4. Spinon band structures for the 12 nearest-neighbor spin-singlet U(1) Ansätze on the pyrochlore lattice listed in Table VI. The spectra are plotted along the high-symmetry momentum paths shown in Fig. 2 for the corresponding parton Brillouin zones. Eight of these states are chiral, while the remaining four are fully symmetric.
FIG. 1. Pyrochlore lattice. The sites occupy the vertices of corner-sharing tetrahedra. The conventional cubic unit cell, with edge length a, is indicated.

FIG. 5. Equal-time spin structure factors of the Gutzwiller-projected nearest-neighbor U(1) chiral spin-liquid Ansätze. The top and middle rows show two-dimensional momentum cuts in the [hhl] and [hk0] planes, respectively. The bottom row shows one-dimensional cuts through the pinch point at G = (0, 0, 2) along the vertical (00kz) and transverse (kk2) directions. While all states exhibit the characteristic pinch-point geometry of a three-dimensional U(1) gauge field, the contrast and apparent sharpness vary substantially across flux sectors because of differing background contributions from matter-field and short-range correlations.

TABLE X. Independent 2nd NN and 3rd NN mean-field parameters and constraints for the (I, S), (IT , S), (I, ST ), and (IT , ST ) U(1) spin liquids. The spin-singlet parameters are underlined. The parameters labeled with a “†” is for the staggered flux state, i.e., the (π, π/2, 0) state. The parameters in color are the further neighbor singlet bonds that can be added to the nearest-neighbor states in Table VI labeled by the same color.

TABLE III. Independent nearest-neighbor (NN) mean-field parameters and symmetry constraints for U(1) chiral spin liquids in the four PSG symmetry classes (I, S), (IT , S), (I, ST ), and (IT , ST ). For each PSG class (first column), the table lists the allowed NN hopping amplitudes together with the algebraic relations imposed by the PSG. The entries specify the independent parameters from which all other NN amplitudes can be generated by symmetry operations. These NN parameters determine the quadratic spinon Hamiltonians and the associated gauge-flux configurations discussed in Sec. III. In the last column, the 12 nearest-neighbor spin-singlet Ansätze are highlighted (see Table VI for further details on these 12 states).

TABLE IV. Independent onsite mean-field parameters for Z2 chiral spin liquids in the four symmetry classes (I, S), (IT , S), (I, ST ), and (IT , ST ) obtained from the PSG classification. Compared with the U(1) case, additional pairing terms are allowed once the invariant gauge group is reduced to Z2. All other onsite terms are fixed by the PSG constraints.

FIG. 3. Nearest-neighbor hopping patterns for the 12 U(1) spin-singlet Ansätze listed in Table VI. Solid bonds represent real hopping amplitudes and dashed bonds purely imaginary ones. Blue (red) bonds denote positive (negative) real hoppings, while a dashed black arrow denotes a purely imaginary hopping with phase +i along the arrow direction.
TABLE XI. Independent 2nd NN mean-field parameters and constraints for the (I, S), (IT , S), (I, ST ), and (IT , ST ) Z2 chiral spin liquids.

TABLE V. Independent nearest-neighbor (NN) mean-field parameters and symmetry constraints for Z2 chiral spin liquids in the four PSG symmetry classes (I, S), (IT , S), (I, ST ), and (IT , ST ). Both hopping and pairing amplitudes are included, as well as the algebraic relations required by symmetry. Spin-singlet parameters are underlined. All other NN matrix elements of the mean-field Hamiltonian follow from these parameters by applying the PSG operations. a Θ = 0, π. § Imcp = √ 3Recp, cp = dp. §§ Imcp = 1√ 3 Recp, cp = dp. ∗ Imcp = 1√ 3 Recp, dh = e π 3 ic∗h, cp = dp. ∗∗ dh = e π 3 ic∗h, Imcp = − √ 3Recp, cp = dp. † Imbh = − 1√ 3 Rebh, Imbp = − 1√ 3 Rebp, Imcp = √ 3Recp, cp = −dp. ‡ Imbp = − √ 3Rebp, Imcp = 1√ 3 Recp, dh = −e π 3 ic∗h, cp = −dp.

TABLE IX. Qualitative ranking of the eight U(1) chiral spin-liquid Ansätze by the relative dominance of the gauge-sector contribution near the pinch point at G = (0, 0, 2). The classification is based on the one-dimensional cuts shown in the bottom row of Fig. 5, comparing the sharpness of the cusp in the vertical (00kz) direction and the degree to which the pinched (kk2) direction is filled in by matter-field and short-range contributions.

TABLE VIII. For the nearest-neighbor spin-1/2 Heisenberg antiferromagnet, the energies per site E/J of the twelve distinct U(1) Ansätze which feature non-vanishing nearest-neighbor singlet amplitudes. The proper and improper symmetries which become chiral or are nonchiral are listed in the last column using the notation of Ref. [40]. The VMC calculations are done on a 2048-site (= 4× 8× 8× 8) cluster which preserves the full symmetry of the pyrochlore lattice. a (0, 0, 0): This Ansatz is labelled as [0, 0, 0] in Ref. [41], and referred to as the uniform state in Ref. [40]. b (π, 0, π): This Ansatz is labelled as [0, 0, π] in Ref. [41], and as (π, π) in Table I of Ref. [40]. c (0, π/2, 0): This Ansatz is referred to as the monopole flux state in Ref. [40]. In Ref. [41], this state is labelled as [π 2 , π 2 , 0] and is referred to as the uniform flux state. d (π, π/2, π): This Ansatz is labelled as (π/2, π) in Table I of Ref. [40]. e (π, π/2, 0): This Ansatz is referred to as the staggered flux state and labelled [π 2 ,−π 2 , 0] in Ref. [41].
